# STM32F030R8 (STM32CubeIDE HAL Project)

## MCU
STM32F030R8 (Cortex-M0, 8KB RAM, 64KB Flash)

## Framework
STM32Cube HAL (generated by STM32CubeMX / STM32CubeIDE)

## Critical rules

### Auto-generated code safety
- NEVER modify code outside:
  USER CODE BEGIN
  USER CODE END
- Do not edit:
  - stm32f0xx_hal_msp.c (except USER CODE sections)
  - MX_* initialization functions (outside user blocks)
  - SystemClock_Config unless explicitly asked

### Architecture style
- HAL-based initialization
- Interrupt-driven peripherals preferred
- Superloop in main()
- State machines for application logic

### Memory constraints
- 8KB RAM limit is strict
- No malloc/free allowed
- Avoid large stack allocations
- Prefer static buffers for DMA/UART

### Interrupt rules
- ISR must be short
- No blocking calls in interrupts
- Use flags or queues (ring buffers preferred)

### HAL usage rules
- Always check HAL return values:
  if (HAL_OK != ...)
- Use HAL_UART_Receive_IT / HAL_UART_Transmit_IT when possible
- Prefer DMA for UART and SPI where available

### Naming conventions
- HAL handles: huart1, hspi1, hadc1, etc.
- Keep CubeMX naming unchanged

### Debugging style
- Use UART debug prints sparingly
- Prefer GPIO toggle for timing analysis

### Performance rules
- Avoid memcpy in loops
- Avoid printf in ISRs
- Minimize context switching between ISR and main loop
